sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application 469 of 2022 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection 25. 2. For hearing of main case. 01.12.2025 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, advocate for the applicant. Messrs. Abdul Rahim Lakhani & Atta M. Qureshi, advocates for the respondent. Pursuant to order dated 20.10.2025, learned counsel for the respondent states that in the interest of all the parties concerned, the impugned order may be set-aside and matter may be remanded back to the learned Tribunal for adjudication afresh expeditiously, preferably, within sixty days. He seeks that pending aforesaid no coercive action be taken against the respondent arising therefrom. Learned counsel for the applicant articulates no cavil in such regard and requests that this reference may be disposed of in the aforesaid terms. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge M. Khan
